title: Minowa & Taito Sightseeing Guide: Old Tokyo Trams, Temples, and Local Food
excerpt: A guide to Minowa's retro charm and its easy access to Asakusa and Ueno, plus where to stay in the neighborhood.
contentHtml:
Minowa is the starting point of the Toden Arakawa Line, Tokyo's last old-style tram, and the neighborhood around it has kept much of its shitamachi character even as the rest of the city has modernized.

Top Sights

Senso-ji Temple & Nakamise-dori — A short trip away in Asakusa, Tokyo's oldest temple and its lively approach street.

Ueno Onshi Park & Zoo — Museums, a zoo, and (in season) some of the city's best cherry blossoms.

The Toden Arakawa Line (Sakura Tram) — A slow, scenic tram ride through neighborhoods most visitors never see.

Yoshiwara Shrine — A small shrine tied to the area's Edo-period history.

Senzoku-dori Shopping Street — A local shopping street with an old-Tokyo feel.

Food

Nearby Minami-Senju and Asakusa offer old-school izakaya and tempura restaurants, while Ueno's Ameyoko market is a good stop for street food and browsing.

Where to Stay

For an easy, affordable base near both tram and subway lines, Plat House Minowa offers a full kitchen, Netflix, and Nintendo Switch. Direct booking beats OTA pricing, with free cancellation up to 7 days out.
